claim: Verified generation with formal math systems achieves better sample efficiency than informal RL approaches, allowing smaller teams to match or exceed frontier lab performance on superhuman tasks
actionable: When training AI systems for mathematical reasoning, prioritize formal verification data (Lean proofs) as training signal over informal RL rewards; Axiom's Putnam perfect score (120/120) vs best informal LLM (DeepSeek 103/120) demonstrates ~20% performance gap from this approach
source_title: Scaling Past Informal AI - Carina Hong, Axiom Math

claim: Lean theorem prover and formal verification tooling enable collaborative AI-human mathematical discovery; the combination unlocks both individual brilliance scaling and team-wide knowledge compounding across research communities
actionable: Structure AI-assisted math research around Lean-based formal verification to enable asynchronous collaboration and proof reuse; treat formal proofs as composable, verifiable knowledge units rather than black-box demonstrations
source_title: Scaling Past Informal AI - Carina Hong, Axiom Math

claim: Recursive decomposition of proof goals into subgoals with learned backtracking eliminates most wall in proof search; scaling inference (not just training) is critical for formal math systems
actionable: Implement proof search with goal decomposition and backtracking learning; prioritize inference-time scaling (search breadth/depth) over training-set size when building math reasoners
source_title: Scaling Past Informal AI - Carina Hong, Axiom Math

claim: The Verina Benchmark (Code With Proof) expects paired code+proof generation; formal systems achieving 99% pass@1 (187/189) vs standard LLMs ~3-22% demonstrates order-of-magnitude advantage of formally-specified objectives
actionable: When evaluating code generation, use structured metrics (formal proof verification, Lean compilation) instead of test-case pass rates; mixed-modality RL (code + informal proof) underperforms relative to code + formal Lean proof pairs
source_title: Scaling Past Informal AI - Carina Hong, Axiom Math

claim: Proof length scales non-linearly with program complexity; current formal systems generate ~20 lines of proof per 1 line of code, creating context-window and tree-scaling challenges
actionable: Plan for proof-to-code ratio when designing formal verification systems; use proof summarization and informalization-then-formalization cycles to manage context constraints
source_title: Scaling Past Informal AI - Carina Hong, Axiom Math

claim: Mathematical discovery (conjecture/construction generation) cannot be done in Lean; it requires separate AI-assisted search before proof formalization, addressing a fundamental gap in automated theorem proving
actionable: Decouple mathematical proof systems into discovery phase (conjecture/construction generation) and verification phase (formal proof); don't expect pure theorem provers to innovate novel mathematics
source_title: Scaling Past Informal AI - Carina Hong, Axiom Math

claim: Combinatorics problems show inherent weakness in formal math systems; creative construction steps transfer poorly from number theory / algebra domains, indicating non-uniform domain transfer
actionable: Recognize that formal math systems will struggle with highly creative problem domains; allocate separate capacity for combinatorics-style problems requiring novel constructions
source_title: Scaling Past Informal AI - Carina Hong, Axiom Math

claim: The specification problem remains unsolved: humans are poor at specifying real-world requirements (finance, safety systems); informal reasoning can propose candidate specs but formal verification cannot generate them
actionable: When applying formal verification to real-world domains, invest in specification refinement (via informal AI proposals + human review) before attempting automated verification
source_title: Scaling Past Informal AI - Carina Hong, Axiom Math

claim: Auto-formalization (converting English specifications to formal statements) is harder than formalization of proofs because informal specs lack numerical grounding; test-case generation partially solves this by providing IO pairs
actionable: When formalizing problems, generate test cases (input/output pairs) first; use these as formal grounding for specification inference before attempting proof generation
source_title: Scaling Past Informal AI - Carina Hong, Axiom Math

claim: Proof systems (Axiom's Axle API) show ~100x speedup in verification via metaprogramming tools; commoditizing formal proof tooling enables collaborative theorem proving across communities
actionable: Use open-source formal verification tooling (Axle for Lean) via API rather than custom verification; this enables faster iteration and interoperability in formal proof systems
source_title: Scaling Past Informal AI - Carina Hong, Axiom Math

claim: Informal verification rewards (human judging, stochastic testing) do not scale to superhuman mathematics; formal verification with mechanical grounding is necessary for scaling beyond human expertise pools
actionable: Treat informal verification as a bottleneck in scaling to superhuman reasoning; invest in formal verification infrastructure if you believe superhuman mathematical reasoning is achievable
source_title: Scaling Past Informal AI - Carina Hong, Axiom Math

claim: RL for formal math with mixed objectives (code in Python + informal proof) underperforms relative to strongly-typed language + formal proof (e.g. Rust + Lean); objective coherence improves learning
actionable: When applying RL to formal reasoning tasks, ensure code and proof are in formally-compatible languages; avoid mixing informal and formal objectives in the same RL loop
source_title: Scaling Past Informal AI - Carina Hong, Axiom Math

claim: Math library formalization (mathlib in Lean) is easier for algebra than analysis; topology/differential geometry lack foundational definitions, limiting system transfer to these domains
actionable: When extending formal math systems to new domains, first check mathlib/Lean coverage of foundational definitions; algebraic domains are easier to formalize than analytical ones
source_title: Scaling Past Informal AI - Carina Hong, Axiom Math

claim: Startup advantage: a small focused team (7 months old, 30 people) can outperform frontier labs on specialized long-horizon problems due to sustained singular focus; frontier labs face organizational pressure for quarterly results
actionable: If pursuing deep technical breakthroughs requiring 12+ month singular focus, consider startup structure over frontier lab roles; organizational incentives matter as much as compute
source_title: Scaling Past Informal AI - Carina Hong, Axiom Math

claim: Lean as a programming language unifies formal proof-writing with functional programming; verification becomes optional and proof becomes executable code (Curry-Howard isomorphism)
actionable: Learn Lean not just for theorem proving but as a dual-use language for code + proof; functional paradigm enables tighter coupling of logic and implementation
source_title: Scaling Past Informal AI - Carina Hong, Axiom Math

claim: Verification should not be framed as a 'bug fix' for hallucination, but as a performance multiplier for scaling brilliance; verified systems achieve superhuman results with lower data budgets than informal RL
actionable: When pitching verification to stakeholders, lead with sample efficiency and performance gains rather than safety; formal verification is a capability lever, not a compliance checkbox
source_title: Scaling Past Informal AI - Carina Hong, Axiom Math

claim: Knowledge graph / knowledge base is a significant competitive moat and time-mode advantage; accumulating verified Lean proofs builds non-linear training signal over time
actionable: If building formal math systems, prioritize long-term data accumulation and knowledge base curation; synthetic data generation compounds over time
source_title: Scaling Past Informal AI - Carina Hong, Axiom Math

claim: Hardware verification (ASIC/GPU) has 1:3-1:4 design-to-verification time ratio; it is a pain point with hundreds of humans per project, creating urgent demand for formal verification automation
actionable: Target hardware verification as a near-term commercial application before attempting broad software verification; hardware margin structure justifies verification tooling costs
source_title: Scaling Past Informal AI - Carina Hong, Axiom Math

claim: Frontier labs (OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepMind) have teams working on formal math (3PF, MiniF2F at OpenAI) but many researchers leave due to strategic direction changes; startups with singular focus have retention advantage
actionable: If pursuing specialized long-term technical problems, consider that frontier labs may deprioritize your area; startup retention of specialized talent may outpace lab capacity
source_title: Scaling Past Informal AI - Carina Hong, Axiom Math

claim: Proof search with tree scaling from 40 nodes to 4000+ nodes is feasible; Axiom Prover (ensemble of post-trained models) handles large proof trees without fundamental compute bottleneck
actionable: When designing proof search systems, plan for 10-100x tree size scaling; inference-time search depth is not fundamentally bounded by current LLM architectures
source_title: Scaling Past Informal AI - Carina Hong, Axiom Math

claim: Distribution shift occurs when extending formal systems across math domains; transfer is strongest between similar domains (number theory → algebra) but weak to orthogonal domains (number theory → topology)
actionable: When scaling formal math systems, test transfer between target domains empirically; don't assume knowledge from well-formalized domains generalizes
source_title: Scaling Past Informal AI - Carina Hong, Axiom Math

claim: Blueprint generation (task decomposition for large proofs) is currently human-driven; automated blueprint generation is a technical bottleneck for large-scale collaborative formal mathematics
actionable: For multi-person formal proof projects, invest in blueprint decomposition tooling; don't expect pure automated systems to generate optimal proof strategies
source_title: Scaling Past Informal AI - Carina Hong, Axiom Math
